steps.title: Identify the student's goal
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize Tecozautla's SIZE. The most effective way to do that is to include a concrete measurement — a specific number that makes the size tangible. Choices that omit the area measurement cannot make size the focal point.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — The municipality of Tecozautla in Hidalgo, Mexico, covers an area of roughly $535 \text{ km}^2$.: Centers the sentence on the area measurement ($535 \text{ km}^2$), directly emphasizing Tecozautla's size with a concrete figure. ✓
  • Choice B — Providing street lighting is just one example of the public services that municipalities provide.: Focuses on municipal services, not on Tecozautla's size at all — the measurement is absent. ✗
  • Choice C — Tecozautla is one of 84 governmental regions, known as municipalities, across Hidalgo.: Focuses on Tecozautla's administrative count among municipalities, not on its physical size. ✗
  • Choice D — Tecozautla—a governmental region in the state of Hidalgo, Mexico—provides many public services to its residents.: Focuses on public services provided, not on the area or size of Tecozautla. ✗
A is correct.
